title: HOLOCAUST SURVIVOR SERVICES - 211info
text: JEWISH FAMILY AND CHILD SERVICE HOLOCAUST SURVIVOR SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support to Holocaust survivors through grant-funded programs that provide homecare assistance, case management, and opportunities for community building. 1221 SW Yamhill Street Suite 301 Portland, OR 97205 Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm, Friday 9am-2pm Email (503) 226-7079 115 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 226-7079 ext 115 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information. Call for appointment. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Photo ID, proof of income, proof of address, and completed intake packet LANGUAGES: English, Russian AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: -MAX red and blue lines. Closest stops: Galleria and SW 10th, Providence Park, Library and SW 9th -Portland Streetcar: 11th and Taylor, 11th and Alder -TriMet bus lines 15 and 63 Services: Aging/Older Adult Support Groups * Holocaust Survivors Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - COUNSELING If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. JEWISH FAMILY AND CHILD SERVICE
